Table 1: Average geodesic errors (↓) of GLASS and baselines on the inter-class benchmark. The best and the second-best are shown in blue and orange respectively.
Table 4: Ablation study on novel components. Average geodesic errors (×100) comparing different design choices on SMAL and TOPKIDS.
Table 3: Average geodesic errors ×100 (↓) of GLASS and baselines on near-isometric benchmarks. The best and the second-best are shown in blue and orange respectively.

Fig. 2: The overall structure of our GLASS. It consists of three key stages: (1) View-consistent feature lifting, where we synthesize coherent textures and lift SDDINO features [48] onto the 3D surface; (2) Language-guided semantic injection, which enriches visual descriptors with linguistic priors using sentence encoder (e.g., SigLip [44]) embeddings derived from zero-shot region proposals; and (3) Regionaware map optimization, where we employ a functional map guided by a novel semantic graph-aware contrastive loss to ensure structural and semantic consistency.
